bibliography:
Austin Young. (2019). Infrastructure as Code: A Comprehensive Guide to Managing Infrastructure as Code. Independently published.
	
	
	AXELOS Limited. (2019). ITIL Foundation: ITIL 4 Edition (ITIL 4 Foundation). TSO.
methodology:
TM1: Expositional: Theoretical exposition according to the syllabus. Assessment with final exam (50%)

TM2: Practical: Hands-on projects with support from the professor. Assessment with a project developed by the student (50%).

 

Important assessment notes:

- Mandatory Minimum score of 9.5 both in the exam and the project.

- Both components of evaluation are mandatory, regardless of the period when they are made.
objectives:
LG1. Acquire the necessary knowledge to plan and design systems and networks in an IT organization.

LG2. Know and experience the essential routines for the operational management of systems and networks in an IT organization.

LG3. Acquire theoretical and practical knowledge in DevOps/DevSecOps.
organicUnit: ECATI - School of Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technologies
programme:
PC1. IT resources and services.

PC2 IT infrastructures and facilities.

PC3. Cloud Infrastructure.

PC4 DevOps/DevSecOps Tools & Orchestration.

PC5. Provision of services and container management.

PC6. Infrastructure as code.

PC7. IT administration and monitoring.

PC8. Good practices of IT management based on ITIL.
presentation: The UC aims to consolidate and expand students' knowledge in the infrastructure and networks component of IT systems. It has a special focus on infrastructure management in the cloud and agile methods for securely provisioning resources.

Época Normal (Avaliação contínua) = Assiduidade (10%) + 2 Testes (20%+20%) + Caso de Uso/Trabalho Prático (50%)

Época Recurso = Caso de Uso/Trabalho Prático (50%) + Exame Teórico (50%)

Época Especial = Caso de Uso/Trabalho Prático (50%) + Exame Teórico (50%)

Nota:  

  • A nota média dos dois testes deverá ser superior a 8.
  • A nota média do trabalho prático deverá ser superior a 9.5.
